c74object.name: void.linkaudio.send~
c74object.digest: Publish audio to an Ableton Link Audio channel and expose Link timing as audio-rate signals
c74object.description: void.linkaudio.send~ publishes one or two channels of audio onto the local Ableton Link Audio network so that any subscribed peer (Ableton Live, TouchDesigner, VCV Rack, another Max patch, etc.) can receive it. In addition, it exposes the current Link session timing as three audio-rate signals (tempo, phase, transport) sampled at the start of every DSP buffer, so that the patch generating the audio to publish can synchronize itself sample-accurately to the shared session. The advertised channel is named via the channel attribute and tagged with the peer name. Stereo or mono publication is selected at creation time via @stereo 0|1 and cannot be changed at runtime, since it determines the number of signal inlets. Built on top of Ableton Link Audio (GPL-2.0-or-later, by Ableton AG); not endorsed by Ableton.

inlet.description: Left audio channel input. Also accepts messages such as bang, info, plus attribute setters (tempo, transport, channel, ...).
Right audio channel input. Present only when the object is created with @stereo 1 (default).

outlet.description: Current Link session tempo in BPM, emitted as an audio-rate signal. Slowly varying. Updated at the start of every DSP buffer from captureAudioSessionState(). Independent of publishing state: this outlet keeps producing meaningful values even when @enable 0 or no sink is currently active.

Sawtooth phase signal in the range [0, quantum), derived from beatAtTime() % quantum at the start of every DSP buffer and advanced linearly across the buffer using beatsPerSample = tempo / 60 / sampleRate. Wraps cleanly at quantum boundaries. Useful for downbeat detection, phase-locked LFOs, and quantized triggering of the audio you are about to publish.

Mirrors the Link session's transport state as a binary audio-rate signal: 1.0 when the session is playing, 0.0 otherwise. Follows the result of session.isPlaying(). Requires startStopSync on the session, which is enabled internally.

Status output. A dictionary <name> message is emitted automatically whenever a meaningful state change occurs (publishing started/stopped, peer count changed) and on demand via the info message. Route through dict.view, dict.unpack, or route to consume it.

method.name: bang
method.description: Re-applies the current state. If publication has not yet started for some reason (e.g. enable just toggled, channel name just changed), this triggers an immediate retry. State changes resulting from the retry will produce an automatic dict dump on the dumpout. This message produces no output by itself.

method.name: info
method.description: Outputs a structured dictionary on the dumpout containing the full current state: enabled, audio_enabled, num_peers, publishing, stereo, channel, peer, frames_published, frames_no_buffer, frames_commit_fail, tempo, transport, beat, phase, quantum, poll, pollinterval, num_audio_channels, plus a sub-dictionary channels listing every channel currently advertised on the network as index -> [channel_name, peer_name].

attribute.name: enable
attribute.description: When set to 1 (default), the object participates in the Link Audio network and publishes its incoming signal under the configured channel name. When set to 0, the channel is removed from the network and audio is dropped silently. Timing outlets keep producing valid values regardless of this setting, as long as the underlying Link session is alive.

attribute.name: stereo
attribute.description: Determines whether the object exposes one (mono) or two (stereo) signal inlets. 1 = stereo (default), 0 = mono. Must be set as a creation argument: @stereo 0 or @stereo 1. Changing this attribute at runtime has no effect on the inlet count.

attribute.name: peer
attribute.digest: Peer Name (this peer's identity on the network)
attribute.description: Identity advertised to other Link Audio peers. Other hosts (Receive nodes, Live, etc.) reference this string when they want to attach specifically to channels published here. Defaults to Max. Pick something distinctive (e.g. Max-Stage1) when several Max instances run on the same network. Note: Link Audio uses a single shared peer name per process, so all Link Audio objects within the same Max instance share whichever value was set most recently.

attribute.name: channel
attribute.description: Name under which this audio stream is advertised on the Link Audio network. Defaults to Max Out. Subscribers (Live, TouchDesigner, VCV Rack, other Max patches) match channels by exact name, so use a stable, descriptive name. When this attribute changes, the publication is restarted under the new name.

attribute.name: quantum
attribute.description: Number of beats per quantum, used by the phase~ outlet (sawtooth period), by the beat / phase values reported via info, and as part of the per-buffer Link timing context attached to each commit. Range 1.0 to 16.0, default 4.0.

attribute.name: tempo
attribute.description: Read/write mirror of the Link session tempo. Reading queries the live session via captureAppSessionState().tempo(), so values reflect changes initiated by any peer (Live, another Max patch, etc.). Writing commits a new tempo back to the session, which propagates to all peers. Range 20.0 to 999.0 BPM. Mirror sync to attrui / pattr happens via the polling clock (default 250 ms).

attribute.name: transport
attribute.description: Read/write mirror of the Link session's transport state (play/stop). Reading queries captureAppSessionState().isPlaying(); writing commits a new state via setIsPlaying(). Propagation between peers requires Start/Stop Sync on each side; this object enables it internally on the shared session. 1 = playing, 0 = stopped.

attribute.name: poll
attribute.description: When set to 1 (default), an internal Max clock fires every pollinterval milliseconds and (1) re-applies the object state for robustness against transient setup issues, (2) refreshes the local tempo and transport attribute storage from the live session and notifies observers (attrui, pattr) via object_attr_touch. Set to 0 to disable the clock; you can then drive retries manually with bang, but external tempo/transport changes will no longer auto-update the attributes.

attribute.name: pollinterval
attribute.description: Interval in milliseconds between automatic state-reapply ticks when poll is enabled. Range 50 to 60000, default 250.
